CREATE SEQUENCE test_seq AS INTEGER START WITH 1 INCREMENT BY 1;
CREATE TABLE test1 (id INT DEFAULT NEXT VALUE FOR test_seq, name VARCHAR(20));
CREATE TABLE test2 (id INT DEFAULT NEXT VALUE FOR test_seq, description VARCHAR(50));
CREATE TABLE combined AS SELECT t1.id AS test_id, t1.name, t2.description FROM test1 AS t1 JOIN test2 AS t2 ON t1.id = t2.id;
INSERT INTO combined (name, description) VALUES (NEXT VALUE FOR test_seq, 'Description from seq');
CREATE TABLE nested_example AS SELECT (SELECT NEXT VALUE FOR test_seq) AS seq_value, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM test1;
CREATE SEQUENCE another_seq AS INTEGER START WITH 100 INCREMENT BY 10;
CREATE TABLE test3 (num INT DEFAULT NEXT VALUE FOR another_seq, info VARCHAR(30));
SELECT t1.num, t1.info FROM test3 AS t1 WHERE t1.num > 100 ORDER BY t1.num DESC;
